title: PhD Vacancy in Contextual (Dis)advantage and Educational Inequality at Utrecht University, Netherlands (Fully Funded)
summary: Are you passionate about understanding how family background, schools, and neighbourhoods collectively shape educational opportunities and social inequality? Utrecht University in the Netherlands is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position in Contextual (Dis)advantage and Educational Inequality within the Department of Sociology, Faculty of Social and Behavioural Sciences. This exciting doctoral project seeks to investigate how different social contexts interact to influence educational outcomes and life chances. The successful PhD candidate will work with high-quality Dutch register data and apply advanced quantitative methods, quasi-experimental designs, and simulation techniques to produce internationally recognized research. About the PhD Project Educational success is influenced by much more than individual ability or family background. Children develop within interconnected environments, including their families, schools, neighbourhoods, and wider social networks. These environments often reinforce or offset one another, creating cumulative advantages or disadvantages throughout life. This research project aims to explore: How children become sorted into different educational and social contexts. How family, school, and neighbourhood characteristics interact. Whether advantages in one context compensate for disadvantages in another. How these contextual interactions contribute to educational inequality. The project will use Dutch administrative register data alongside advanced statistical techniques to generate evidence that can inform educational policy and social research. Responsibilities The selected PhD candidate will: Conduct literature reviews. Analyse large-scale administrative datasets. Publish research in international scientific journals. Write and complete a doctoral dissertation. Present research at national and international conferences. Participate actively within the Department of Sociology. Contribute to teaching activities for up to 10% of working time. Benefits Successful applicants will receive: A full-time PhD appointment (36–40 hours per week). Initial one-year contract, extendable to four years upon satisfactory performance. Monthly gross salary ranging from €3,059 (Year 1) to €3,881 (Year 4) . 8% holiday allowance. 8.3% year-end bonus. Pension scheme. Partially paid parental leave. Professional development opportunities. Flexible employment conditions. Access to sports and cultural activities offered by Utrecht University. Opportunity to conduct internationally impactful research at one of Europe s leading universities. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should: Hold (or be close to completing) a Master s degree in Sociology, Interdisciplinary Social Science, or a related discipline. Demonstrate strong interest in socio-economic and ethnic inequality. Have experience with advanced quantitative research methods or willingness to learn. Preferably have experience working with large and complex datasets. Enjoy academic research and scientific writing. Have excellent organizational and communication skills. Possess strong English writing and presentation skills. Reading proficiency in Dutch is considered an advantage. About Utrecht University Utrecht University is one of Europe s leading research universities and is internationally recognized for excellence in education and research. The Faculty of Social and Behavioural Sciences hosts nearly 7,000 students and over 1,100 academic and professional staff working across sociology, psychology, educational sciences, anthropology, and related disciplines. Application Process Applicants should prepare: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Motivation letter Overview of Master s courses and grades Applications should be submitted through Utrecht University s official recruitment portal. title: Fully Funded Curtin University PhD Opportunity 2026: Research the Long-Term Impact of Neonatal Intensive Care in Australia (RTP Scholarship Available)
summary: Curtin University is inviting Expressions of Interest from highly motivated domestic and international applicants for an exciting PhD research opportunity investigating the long-term effects of neonatal intensive care on newborns and their families. The project offers candidates the opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research using linked population-level administrative datasets from Western Australia while receiving advanced training in causal inference, health economics, econometrics, and health policy analysis. Successful applicants identified as the preferred candidate may also be considered for a prestigious Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship , one of Australia s most competitive postgraduate research scholarships. This research is particularly suited for candidates interested in health economics, epidemiology, biostatistics, econometrics, public health, or quantitative policy analysis. Application Period: 1 July 2026 – 31 July 2026 About the Research Project Every year, hospitals face difficult decisions regarding the level of medical care provided to very small newborn babies. One important clinical threshold used in Australian hospitals is a birthweight of 1,500 grams . Infants born weighing less than 1,500 grams typically receive: More intensive neonatal monitoring. Additional specialist care. Longer hospital stays. Greater access to neonatal intensive care services. Meanwhile, babies born just above the threshold often receive comparatively less intensive treatment despite being extremely similar medically. This creates what researchers describe as a natural experiment . Because babies born just below and just above 1,500 grams are almost identical except for the treatment they receive, researchers can estimate the true impact of additional neonatal care without many of the biases that affect traditional observational studies. The project aims to determine whether the additional medical care provided below the 1,500-gram threshold leads to better outcomes not only for newborns but also for their wider families. Why This Research Matters Providing neonatal intensive care is among the most resource-intensive services offered within modern healthcare systems. Despite the substantial investment required, an important question remains: How much additional benefit does intensive neonatal care actually provide? Traditional studies struggle to answer this because babies receiving more care are usually those who are sicker. This makes it difficult to distinguish: the effect of medical treatment, and the severity of the baby s initial condition. This project overcomes that challenge by comparing infants born immediately below and above the 1,500-gram threshold, where treatment intensity changes but the babies themselves remain remarkably similar. This approach enables researchers to estimate the causal effects of additional neonatal care with far greater credibility. Research Background The study uses linked administrative records covering births across Western Australia. The primary data source is the Midwives Notification System , which records birthweight to the exact gram. The linked datasets then follow each child through multiple stages of life, including: Hospital admissions Emergency department visits Mortality records School records Where available, the data also include linked records for: Mothers Brothers and sisters This comprehensive linkage allows researchers to examine outcomes across the entire population rather than relying on sample surveys. Research Aims The project has two primary aims. Aim 1: Measure the Effect of Additional Neonatal Care The first objective is to estimate the local causal effect of the increased neonatal care received by babies born just below the 1,500-gram threshold. Researchers will examine differences in: Hospital treatment intensity Length of hospital stay Medical costs Hospital readmissions Survival during the first year of life Long-term health Educational performance Aim 2: Examine Wider Family Benefits A distinctive feature of this research is its focus on family outcomes. Rather than studying only the treated child, the project investigates whether neonatal care produces broader benefits affecting: Maternal health after childbirth Educational outcomes of siblings This broader perspective may reveal additional social and economic returns that are often overlooked in healthcare evaluations. Research Objectives During the PhD, the successful candidate will: Construct an analysis-ready dataset using linked population records. Combine information from: Birth records Hospital datasets Emergency department records Death records School outcome databases Maternal records (where available) Sibling records (where linkage permits) Apply modern econometric methods to estimate causal effects around the 1,500-gram threshold. Conduct rigorous statistical testing to validate the research design. Estimate the effects of additional neonatal care on: Infant survival Hospital utilization Healthcare expenditure Long-term physical health Educational achievement Investigate wider family outcomes, including: Maternal health Siblings educational performance Research Methodology The project employs advanced applied econometric techniques commonly used in health economics and policy evaluation. The study relies on a regression discontinuity design (RDD), which exploits the treatment change occurring at the 1,500-gram birthweight threshold. Key methodological components include: Testing whether babies cannot be deliberately sorted around the cutoff. Verifying that predetermined characteristics remain continuous across the threshold. Measuring differences in treatment intensity. Estimating causal effects of neonatal care. Performing robustness and sensitivity analyses. Students will gain extensive experience in modern causal inference methods widely used in economics and epidemiology. Expected Outcomes The project seeks to generate evidence regarding: Child Outcomes Survival during infancy Hospital admissions Emergency department use Healthcare costs Long-term health Educational achievement Family Outcomes Maternal health following childbirth Educational outcomes of siblings Family spillover effects Why This Project Is Significant The significance of this research extends beyond neonatal medicine. It contributes to several important fields, including: Health economics Public health Neonatal medicine Health policy Education policy Causal inference Population health research If wider family benefits are identified, current evaluations of neonatal intensive care may underestimate its true value. The findings could influence: Healthcare funding decisions Hospital planning Neonatal service delivery Resource allocation National health policy The research therefore has important implications for both clinical practice and public policy. Internship Opportunity The successful PhD candidate is expected to complete an internship during the project. The internship will be arranged through the research group s existing partnerships within Australia s healthcare sector. Potential host organizations include: Western Australia Country Health Service Fiona Stanley Hospital Other collaborating health system partners The internship aims to provide practical experience in: Healthcare planning Health service delivery Policy implementation Knowledge translation Engagement with decision-makers Students will have the opportunity to share their research findings with professionals involved in planning and funding neonatal and maternity services. Scholarship Information Applicants selected as the preferred candidate may be considered for a Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship . The RTP Scholarship is one of Australia s flagship postgraduate research funding schemes and may provide financial support throughout the PhD program, subject to eligibility and university selection processes. Read more about RTP Scholarship HERE Who Should Apply? Curtin University is seeking applicants who demonstrate: Strong quantitative ability. Interest in health economics and health policy. Careful data management skills. Excellent statistical reasoning. Motivation to undertake independent research. Applicants with backgrounds in the following disciplines are encouraged to apply: Economics Econometrics Biostatistics Epidemiology Public Health Statistics Health Economics Data Science Other related quantitative fields Experience with the following is advantageous: Stata R Administrative datasets Applied econometrics Modern causal inference methods Applicants must also be eligible for admission into a PhD program at Curtin University. Benefits of This Opportunity Successful candidates will benefit from: Potential RTP Scholarship funding. Access to one of Australia s richest linked health datasets. Advanced training in causal inference. Experience working with population-level administrative data. Mentorship from experienced researchers. Internship opportunities with leading Australian healthcare organizations. Opportunities to influence health policy and neonatal care planning. Development of high-demand quantitative research skills. Strong preparation for careers in academia, government, healthcare, and policy research. Visit HERE for more information on Fully Funded Curtin University PhD Opportunity 2026: Research the Long-Term Impact of Neonatal Intensive Care in Australia (RTP Scholarship Available) How to Apply Interested applicants should submit an Expression of Interest during Curtin University s Central Scholarship Round. Application Period 1 July 2026 – 31 July 2026 Enquiries Applicants seeking further information about the project may contact: Dr. Marshall Makate Email: Marshall.Makate@curtin.edu.au To formally apply submit an Expressions of Interest directly to Dr. Marshall Makate during the Central Scholarship application period. Important Deadline Applications open: 1 July 2026 Application deadline: 31 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare their Expression of Interest well before the closing date to allow sufficient time for review and scholarship consideration. Conclusion This PhD opportunity at Curtin University offers an exceptional platform for aspiring researchers seeking to make meaningful contributions to neonatal health, health economics, and evidence-based public policy. By leveraging one of Australia s most comprehensive linked administrative health datasets, the project will generate valuable insights into how neonatal intensive care influences not only infant survival and long-term outcomes but also the wellbeing of mothers and siblings. With the possibility of receiving a prestigious RTP Scholarship, undertaking an industry-connected internship, and developing advanced expertise in causal inference and quantitative research methods, this opportunity provides outstanding academic and professional development. Candidates passionate about improving healthcare systems through rigorous data-driven research are strongly encouraged to submit their Expression of Interest before 31 July 2026 . title: CircularEconomy4Ghana Innovation Challenge 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Agritech Startups and Entrepreneurs to Scale Circular Economy Solutions in Ghana
summary: The CircularEconomy4Ghana Innovation Challenge 2026 is officially accepting applications from innovative startups, SMEs, and entrepreneurs developing transformative agritech and circular economy solutions that can contribute to a more sustainable, resilient, and food-secure Ghana. Officially launched during the Ghana–Italy Circular Economy Dialogue , this prestigious innovation challenge is designed to identify and support promising early-stage businesses that are using technology, innovation, and sustainable business models to address agricultural challenges while advancing the circular economy. The initiative is led by the Alliance of Bioversity International and CIAT through the CGIAR Accelerate for Impact Platform (A4IP) and implemented in collaboration with the International Water Management Institute (IWMI) , UNIDO ITPO Italy , and E4Impact Foundation . The challenge is endorsed by Ghana s National Entrepreneurship Innovation Programme (NEIP) and supported by the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation . If you are an entrepreneur building innovative solutions that improve agriculture, reduce waste, increase resource efficiency, or strengthen food systems, this is an outstanding opportunity to receive mentorship, technical support, business acceleration, investor exposure, and market access. About the CircularEconomy4Ghana Innovation Challenge The CircularEconomy4Ghana Innovation Challenge was created to empower innovators who are developing practical technologies and business solutions capable of transforming Ghana s agricultural sector through circular economy principles. Rather than focusing solely on innovation, the challenge aims to help entrepreneurs refine their technologies, validate their products, access commercial markets, and scale their businesses to create long-term environmental, social, and economic impact. Participants will receive structured support that enables them to transition from promising ideas into market-ready businesses capable of serving farmers, agribusinesses, and agricultural value chains across Ghana. The initiative forms part of the CGIAR Food Frontiers Security Science Program and MFL , supporting sustainable agriculture and innovation across developing economies. Objectives of the Challenge The program seeks to: Promote circular economy solutions within Ghana s agricultural sector. Improve national food security. Build climate resilience through sustainable agricultural innovation. Support entrepreneurs developing scalable agritech technologies. Help innovators validate products with real users. Increase market readiness for early-stage businesses. Connect startups with investors and commercial partners. Encourage sustainable business models that benefit farmers and agricultural communities. Priority Innovation Areas Applicants should be developing solutions that address one or more of the following priority areas: 1. Innovative Financing and Financial Access for Circular Agri-Enterprises Solutions that improve financial inclusion and create innovative financing mechanisms for businesses operating within the circular agricultural economy. 2. Digital Advisory and Knowledge-Sharing Systems for Circular Agriculture Technologies that provide farmers and agricultural businesses with digital advisory services, knowledge-sharing platforms, and data-driven decision-making tools. 3. Waste Segregation, Collection, and Valorization Systems Innovations that reduce agricultural waste while transforming waste into valuable products, resources, or new business opportunities. 4. Nutrient Recovery and Circular Soil Fertility Solutions Solutions that recover nutrients from agricultural by-products and improve soil fertility through sustainable circular practices. 5. Traceability, Data, and Supply Chain Efficiency for Circular Value Chains Technologies that improve transparency, traceability, logistics, and operational efficiency throughout agricultural supply chains. 6. Inclusive Circular Business Models for Youth and Women Business models that create inclusive opportunities while empowering youth and women within Ghana s agricultural value chains. Benefits of the CircularEconomy4Ghana Innovation Challenge Selected participants will gain access to an extensive package of technical, entrepreneurial, and commercial support. Benefits include: A deep understanding of Ghana s agricultural ecosystem. A fully funded four-day immersive bootcamp in Accra, Ghana. Tailored business mentorship. Product validation support. Pilot testing with end-users. Market access opportunities. Strategic connections with agribusinesses and ecosystem partners. Expert guidance from CGIAR scientists. Mentorship from UNIDO experts. A fully sponsored three-month accelerator programme. Entrepreneurial training. Technical assistance. Support in building a strong go-to-market strategy. Opportunities to meet investors. Exposure to commercial partners. Support to refine, test, and scale innovative agritech solutions. Who Can Apply? The challenge is looking for ambitious innovators with promising agricultural technologies and circular economy solutions. Applicants should meet the following requirements: Be a startup, SME, or entrepreneur. Have an early-stage agritech innovation. Possess a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP). Ideally have already tested the MVP with end-users. Be actively fundraising at either the pre-seed or seed stage. Address real market needs. Respond to local agricultural challenges. Align with at least one of the identified priority areas. Demonstrate measurable benefits for smallholder farmers and agricultural value chain actors. Why You Should Apply The CircularEconomy4Ghana Innovation Challenge goes beyond a traditional startup competition. Participants receive practical business development support, expert mentorship, investor engagement opportunities, technology validation, and direct exposure to Ghana s growing agricultural innovation ecosystem. Whether your innovation focuses on digital agriculture, waste management, sustainable farming, soil health, agricultural finance, or supply chain efficiency, this programme provides the resources needed to transform an early-stage innovation into a scalable business. Organizations Behind the Initiative The challenge is led and supported by several internationally recognized organizations working together to strengthen agricultural innovation and sustainability. These include: Alliance of Bioversity International and CIAT CGIAR Accelerate for Impact Platform (A4IP) International Water Management Institute (IWMI) UNIDO ITPO Italy E4Impact Foundation National Entrepreneurship Innovation Programme (NEIP) Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation World Trade Centre Accra is also supporting the initiative in promoting innovation, sustainability, agritech, and international collaboration. Application Deadline Application Deadline: 08 August 2026 (23:59 UTC) Late applications may not be considered, so interested innovators are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. title: Fully Funded PhD Positions in Applied Macroeconomics and Political Economy at ETH Zurich, Switzerland 2026
summary: Are you passionate about macroeconomics, public finance, international taxation, or political economy? Do you aspire to pursue a world-class doctoral degree at one of Europe s leading universities while contributing to impactful research that influences economic policy? ETH Zurich is inviting outstanding candidates from around the world to apply for one or more PhD Positions in Applied Macroeconomics and Political Economy at the prestigious KOF Swiss Economic Institute. This doctoral opportunity provides researchers with the chance to conduct cutting-edge empirical research, collaborate with internationally recognized economists, participate in global conferences, and earn a PhD from one of the world s highest-ranked universities. The positions are designed for ambitious graduates who want to investigate real-world economic challenges using advanced econometric methods and large-scale datasets while developing expertise that can shape future economic policies. Applications are open until 30 September 2026 . About ETH Zurich ETH Zurich is among the world s leading universities specializing in science, technology, engineering, economics, and innovation. Located in Zurich, Switzerland, the institution has earned a global reputation for: Academic excellence Groundbreaking scientific research International collaboration Policy-relevant innovation Strong industry partnerships With over 30,000 students and researchers representing more than 120 countries, ETH Zurich provides an international environment that promotes independent thinking, innovation, and academic excellence. The university is also home to the internationally respected KOF Swiss Economic Institute , Switzerland s premier institute for applied economic research. About the PhD Opportunity The Chair of Applied Macroeconomics, affiliated with the Department of Management, Technology and Economics (D-MTEC), is seeking talented doctoral researchers to join its expanding research team. Successful applicants will become members of both: The Chair of Applied Macroeconomics KOF Swiss Economic Institute The program combines academic excellence with policy-oriented research, allowing doctoral candidates to investigate important economic questions that influence governments, international organizations, and policymakers. Rather than assigning fixed dissertation topics, supervisors will work closely with each doctoral researcher to design an individualized research project that aligns with the student s interests and career goals. Research Areas The research focuses on empirical macroeconomics and policy-relevant economic issues using advanced quantitative methods. Potential research themes include: 1. Globalization and Fiscal Policy Researchers may investigate: Global economic integration International policy competition Fiscal sustainability Government spending Public finance reforms 2. International Taxation One PhD position is specifically aimed at applicants interested in taxation within a global economy. Possible research topics include: Corporate taxation Tax havens Multinational profit shifting International tax competition Political economy of tax rules Labour-market effects of taxation Distributional effects of international taxation 3. Political Economy Possible research areas include: Electoral incentives Political institutions Economic freedom Public policy design Government accountability Information and policymaking 4. Applied Macroeconomics Students may research: Business cycles Inflation Monetary policy Economic forecasting Productivity Long-term economic growth 5. International Economics Topics include: Global trade International markets Cross-border investment Economic integration International policy coordination 6. Migration and Labour Markets Research may examine: Labour-market dynamics Migration economics Household behaviour Employment outcomes Demographic changes 7. Corruption and Governance Candidates may also investigate: Institutional quality Governance reforms Corruption Policy effectiveness Economic development Types of Data Used Researchers will gain experience working with diverse datasets, including: Firm-level data Household data Country-level datasets Administrative data Survey data Geospatial datasets International datasets Text-based datasets Microeconomic databases Macroeconomic databases Research Methods Doctoral researchers will develop expertise in modern econometric methods, including: Panel-data analysis Time-series econometrics Causal inference Quantitative analysis Statistical modelling Forecasting techniques Advanced empirical research methods Responsibilities of Successful Candidates As a doctoral researcher, you will: Develop an original dissertation. Design innovative research projects. Formulate research questions. Conduct empirical analyses. Publish academic papers. Collect and manage research datasets. Document research findings. Present work at seminars. Attend international academic conferences. Participate in workshops. Join research meetings. Complete doctoral coursework. Contribute to limited teaching activities when required. Collaborate with fellow researchers. Supervision Successful candidates will receive academic supervision from: Prof. Dr. Jan-Egbert Sturm Dr. Enea Baselgia (depending on the research area) The supervisory team will provide continuous mentorship throughout the doctoral journey. Academic Environment The Chair emphasizes: Collaboration Academic freedom Research excellence Policy relevance Innovation Interdisciplinary learning Researchers are encouraged to exchange ideas, participate in seminars, and build strong academic networks. Benefits of the PhD Position Successful candidates will enjoy numerous benefits, including: Opportunity to earn a PhD from ETH Zurich. Employment at one of Europe s leading universities. Membership in the renowned KOF Swiss Economic Institute. Competitive salary according to ETH Zurich regulations. International research exposure. Access to leading economists and researchers. Participation in international conferences. Opportunities to present research globally. Professional mentoring. Career development support. Access to advanced datasets. Strong interdisciplinary collaboration. Research workshops. Academic seminars. Networking with international scholars. Opportunity to build an international academic profile. Potential extension to complete a four- to five-year doctorate. Employment Conditions The successful applicants will receive: Initial appointment of 18 months. Possibility of extension based on satisfactory progress. Expected total PhD duration of approximately 4–5 years. Competitive gross salary under ETH Zurich regulations. Negotiable starting date. Who Should Apply? ETH Zurich welcomes applicants from diverse academic and personal backgrounds. Applicants should have: A completed Master s degree in Economics or a closely related discipline before the position begins. Strong interest in empirical economic research. Interest in macroeconomics. Interest in political economy. Interest in public finance. Interest in international economics. Strong quantitative skills. Knowledge of econometrics. Knowledge of statistics. Experience using Stata, Python, or R. Excellent English communication skills. Interest in collaborative research. Applicants from quantitative disciplines such as: Statistics Data Science Political Science Finance Public Policy are also encouraged to apply if they possess sufficient economics training. Preferred Skills Although not mandatory, the following are considered advantageous: Panel-data methods Time-series econometrics Causal inference Large microeconomic datasets International taxation Tax competition Fiscal federalism Political economy Research career aspirations German language skills (optional) Commitment to Diversity ETH Zurich strongly promotes: Equal opportunities Diversity Inclusion Respect Academic freedom The university particularly encourages applications from women and individuals from groups traditionally underrepresented in economics. Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare: Letter of motivation explaining research interests. Curriculum Vitae (without a photograph). Writing sample (Master s thesis, research paper, or equivalent). Complete academic transcript. Copies of degrees and certificates. Contact details of two or three referees. How to Apply Interested candidates must submit their applications through the official ETH Zurich online application portal. Applications submitted by: Email Postal mail will not be accepted. APPLYHERE Application Deadline The deadline to apply is 30 September 2026. Late applications will not be considered. Why You Should Apply This PhD opportunity offers far more than doctoral study—it provides a platform to become part of one of Europe s most respected economic research communities. Students will gain advanced research skills, publish influential work, collaborate with internationally recognized scholars, and contribute to solving pressing global economic challenges. Whether your ambition is an academic career, work with international organizations, government institutions, central banks, or economic policy think tanks, this doctoral program provides exceptional preparation and international recognition. Visit HERE for more information about the Fully Funded PhD Positions in Applied Macroeconomics and Political Economy at ETH Zurich, Switzerland 2026 Conclusion The ETH Zurich PhD Positions in Applied Macroeconomics and Political Economy represent an outstanding opportunity for talented graduates seeking to build a research career in economics. Through rigorous training, world-class supervision, international collaboration, and access to one of Europe s leading economic research institutes, successful candidates will develop the expertise needed to influence academic research and economic policymaking worldwide. If you have a strong quantitative background, a passion for applied economic research, and the desire to contribute to evidence-based policymaking, this doctoral program could be the ideal next step in your academic journey. Be sure to submit your complete application before 30 September 2026 . title: Call for Proposals: Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung Announces $10,000 Grants to Support Feminist Projects in Syria and Iraq (2026)
summary: The Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung (RLS) Regional Office in Beirut has officially opened applications for its 2026 Call for Proposals aimed at supporting feminist projects in Syria and Iraq. Through this funding opportunity, the organization seeks to strengthen grassroots feminist movements, promote gender justice, combat gender-based violence, and empower local organizations working in some of the region s most challenging humanitarian and conflict-affected environments. With grants of up to USD 10,000 per selected project , this initiative provides financial support to innovative feminist initiatives that address the realities faced by women and marginalized communities living amid conflict, displacement, and social fragmentation. Organizations, networks, grassroots groups, and qualified individuals are invited to submit proposals that contribute to building stronger, more inclusive, and resilient communities. About Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung (RLS) The Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung (RLS) is a German political foundation affiliated with the German political party Die Linke (The Left Party) . The foundation focuses on political education, democracy, social justice, progressive public policy, and international cooperation. Its Beirut Regional Office, established in 2017, works extensively across: Lebanon Syria Iraq The office also publishes research and analysis covering developments in Iran. Through partnerships with local organizations, RLS supports initiatives that promote equality, social justice, democratic participation, and human rights throughout the region. Why This Grant Matters Communities across Syria and Iraq continue to face enormous challenges after years of armed conflict, political instability, economic crises, and mass displacement. These challenges include: Social fragmentation Weak community trust Ethnic and sectarian divisions Gender-based violence Displacement and migration Limited civic participation Human rights violations Economic hardship Marginalization of vulnerable communities Although conflict affects everyone, women, girls, LGBTQI+ persons, and marginalized populations often experience disproportionate levels of violence, discrimination, exclusion, and insecurity. Through this Call for Proposals, RLS seeks to strengthen feminist responses that address these challenges while encouraging solidarity, inclusion, and long-term community resilience. Objectives of the Grant Applicants are encouraged to design projects that contribute to one or more of the following objectives. 1. Address Gendered Impacts of Conflict Projects should focus on: The unique challenges women face during conflict Violence against women and girls Gender discrimination Human rights violations Protection of marginalized communities 2. Promote Inclusion Successful proposals should encourage: Participation of marginalized populations Women s leadership Community decision-making Equal representation Inclusive local governance 3. Build Community Solidarity Applicants are encouraged to develop initiatives that strengthen: Collective care Mutual support systems Community safety Social cohesion Protection networks 4. Strengthen Feminist Organizations Projects may focus on increasing the capacity of: Women s organizations Grassroots feminist initiatives Human rights defenders Local activists Community leaders 5. Conduct Research and Documentation RLS welcomes proposals involving: Research studies Policy analysis Documentation of conflict impacts Gender-based violence research Feminist peacebuilding studies Research should include practical recommendations for improving gender justice and inclusion. 6. Encourage Cross-Border Collaboration Applicants are encouraged to build partnerships between: Syrian and Iraqi organizations Host and displaced communities Local feminist movements Regional networks Expected Project Outcomes Applicants should clearly define the results their projects will achieve. Possible outcomes include: Community-Based Programs Examples include: Safe spaces Protection mechanisms Campaigns against gender-based violence Mutual aid programs Legal support Psychosocial support services Capacity Building Projects may include: Feminist leadership development Organizational strengthening Security training Advocacy planning Collective care approaches Research and Documentation Potential outputs include: Research reports Policy papers Case studies Conflict documentation Human rights reports Recommendations for policymakers Development of Practical Tools Applicants may develop: Training manuals Toolkits Peacebuilding models Community protection methodologies Gender inclusion frameworks Networking and Partnerships Projects can also strengthen collaboration among: Feminist organizations Women s groups Grassroots initiatives Regional networks Displaced and host communities Who Can Apply? The call welcomes applications from: Individuals Grassroots feminist initiatives Women-led organizations LGBTQI+ groups Civil society organizations Community-led organizations Coalitions Networks Applicants must be actively working in Syria or Iraq. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should demonstrate experience in areas such as: Feminist organizing Gender justice Women s rights Intersectional activism Human rights Peacebuilding Community organizing Local Knowledge Applicants should understand: Local conflict dynamics Social and cultural realities Political context Displacement challenges Community needs Commitment to Core Values Projects should reflect values including: Social justice Equality Human rights Inclusion Solidarity Non-discrimination Intersectional feminism Project Management Capacity Applicants should provide: Clear methodology Work plan Timeline Security considerations Monitoring approach Financial Readiness Applications should include: Realistic budget Transparent financial planning Clear explanation of proposed expenses Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following: 1. Project Proposal Maximum length: Five pages (excluding annexes) The proposal should include: Background Context Objectives Target beneficiaries Activities Methodology Expected outcomes Timeline Risk assessment 2. Detailed Budget Applicants should explain: Planned expenditures Budget allocation Cost justification 3. Curriculum Vitae (CV) Include: Applicant CV Previous relevant experience Team member biographies (if applicable) Evaluation Criteria Applications will be assessed according to the following criteria: Evaluation Area Weight Relevance to feminist objectives and gender justice 20% Quality, clarity, and feasibility of the proposal 15% Applicant s experience and understanding of local context 20% Realistic and justified budget 25% Sustainability, resilience, and long-term impact 20% Grant Amount Each selected project will receive: USD 10,000 Project Duration Projects should run between: August 2026 – December 2026 Geographic Scope Projects may: Operate in Syria Operate in Iraq Be jointly implemented across both countries Cross-border and regional collaboration is highly encouraged. Preferred Partnerships RLS particularly welcomes projects that encourage collaboration between: Different communities Host and displaced populations Local and regional feminist organizations Networks working across borders Language Requirements Applications may be submitted in: English Arabic How to Apply Applicants should email all required documents to: info.beirut@rosalux.org Use the following subject line: Call for Project Proposal – Feminism NOTE : Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Learn more about this application HERE Application Deadline: July 25, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their proposals early to ensure all required documentation is submitted before the deadline. Conclusion The Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung s 2026 Call for Proposals represents an important opportunity for feminist organizations, grassroots initiatives, researchers, and civil society actors in Syria and Iraq to secure funding for impactful projects promoting gender justice, community resilience, and social inclusion. Beyond financial support, this initiative encourages collaborative approaches that strengthen local leadership, empower marginalized communities, address gender-based violence, and contribute to long-term peacebuilding in conflict-affected societies. Organizations with innovative, community-centered ideas are encouraged to submit well-developed proposals before the application deadline to maximize their chances of selection. title: Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations 2026 – Fully Funded Youth Festival in the USA for Young Leaders Aged 18–24
summary: Young people are already leading change in their communities, classrooms, workplaces, creative industries, and entrepreneurial ventures. Yet, many national conversations about the future rarely include those who will actually shape and experience it. To address this gap, the Aspen Institute is launching Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations , the first youth-centered event in the 21-year history of the Aspen Ideas Festival. Scheduled to take place from November 13–16, 2026 , in Bentonville, Arkansas , this groundbreaking event is designed entirely by and for young people. The four-day gathering will bring together 350 outstanding young adults from across the United States to engage in meaningful conversations, explore today s most pressing issues, exchange perspectives, and collaborate on ideas that can influence the future. One of the most attractive features of the event is that accepted participants attend completely free of charge , with travel, accommodation, meals, and all programming fully funded. Application Deadline Deadline: July 29, 2026 About Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations is a new initiative created by the Aspen Institute as an extension of the renowned Aspen Ideas Festival. For more than 21 years , the Aspen Ideas Festival has served as a platform where influential thinkers, innovators, policymakers, entrepreneurs, artists, and leaders gather to discuss the world s biggest challenges. For the first time, this prestigious platform is creating an event that belongs entirely to young adults. Rather than asking young people to observe conversations, Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations places them at the center of every discussion. The event has been developed alongside the Center for Rising Generations Youth Advisory Council , ensuring that every discussion, workshop, and experience reflects issues that matter most to today s generation. Why Attend Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations? Participants from diverse faith traditions and worldviews—including religious and non-religious perspectives—will discuss: Faith Values Identity Purpose Belonging Personal growth Dialogue across differences Why This Festival Matters Young people are already: Launching businesses Creating artistic movements Building communities Organizing civic initiatives Solving local challenges Leading conversations about the future Aspen Ideas: Rising Generations provides a national platform where these efforts can grow through dialogue, collaboration, and shared learning. Who Should Apply? title: Youth Endowment Fund Violence Against Women and Girls Prevention Programme: Objective 2 Grant 2026 for Organizations Delivering Early Intervention in Educational Settings.
summary: The Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) has opened applications for its Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG) Prevention Programme – Objective 2 , offering funding to organizations working to address inappropriate and problematic attitudes and behaviours surrounding gender norms and relationships among children and young people in educational settings across England. This funding opportunity is part of a broader three-year programme aimed at identifying and expanding evidence-based interventions that help prevent violence against women and girls before harmful behaviours escalate. Successful applicants will receive funding not only to deliver their intervention but also to participate in an independent evaluation that will contribute to national evidence on effective prevention strategies. Organizations with proven interventions, strong delivery experience, and a commitment to improving young people s understanding of respectful relationships are encouraged to apply. About the Youth Endowment Fund Violence Against Women and Girls Prevention Programme The Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) was established in 2019 with a £200 million endowment and a ten-year mandate from the UK Home Office to identify and support effective strategies that prevent children and young people from becoming involved in violence. Violence against women and girls remains a significant challenge across society. Preventing harmful behaviours before they develop into serious violence requires early intervention, education, and evidence-based support systems. This funding opportunity directly supports the UK Government s Freedom from Violence and Abuse Strategy , published in December 2025, particularly its commitment to prevention and early intervention among young people. Objective 2 specifically focuses on helping schools and educational settings respond effectively when children and young people begin displaying concerning attitudes and behaviours relating to gender norms and relationships. Purpose of the Grant The programme aims to fund targeted interventions that identify and respond to emerging inappropriate and problematic attitudes and behaviours before they escalate into serious forms of abuse or violence. Rather than waiting until statutory services such as policing, children s social care, or youth justice become necessary, the programme supports early intervention approaches that can positively influence young people s attitudes and behaviours. The overall goal is to strengthen educational settings so they are better equipped to identify, manage, and prevent harmful behaviours while promoting healthy, respectful, and equal relationships. What the Grant Will Fund The Youth Endowment Fund is looking for organizations delivering targeted interventions that work with children and young people aged 10–18 years. Projects should primarily focus on supporting pupils displaying early signs of problematic attitudes and behaviours relating to gender norms and relationships. Funded interventions may include: Direct support for children and young people displaying concerning behaviours. Programmes promoting healthy, respectful, and equal relationships. Behaviour change interventions. Early identification tools and resources. Training programmes for teachers and school staff. Support systems that strengthen schools responses to emerging concerns. Educational resources that improve safeguarding practices. Collaborative work between schools and community partners where there is a strong educational link. Projects may involve community-based delivery where appropriate, provided there is a clear connection with educational institutions. Focus on Secondary Prevention This funding round specifically supports secondary prevention . Secondary prevention involves identifying early warning signs and providing targeted support before behaviours become severe enough to require intervention from statutory agencies. Examples include: Addressing unhealthy gender stereotypes. Challenging problematic relationship attitudes. Preventing harmful behaviours from escalating. Supporting young people at risk of developing abusive behaviours. Building healthier relationship skills. Evidence-Based Interventions Required Applicants should note that the Youth Endowment Fund is not seeking entirely new programmes developed from scratch . Instead, proposals should demonstrate that interventions are already informed by: Existing research. Proven theory. Academic literature. Established practice. Previous implementation experience. Evidence showing promise. However, interventions may still require refinement or adaptation before undergoing larger-scale evaluation. Independent Evaluation An important feature of this grant is that every funded project will also undergo an independent evaluation. The Youth Endowment Fund will appoint external evaluators who will work alongside funded organizations to: Co-design evaluation methods. Assess implementation quality. Measure outcomes. Generate evidence of effectiveness. Inform future policy and investment decisions. Funding is only available for projects willing to participate fully in this evaluation process. Standalone project delivery without evaluation will not be supported. Evaluation Scale Most funded interventions are expected to participate in either: Feasibility studies Pilot evaluations Applicants should propose: Appropriate evaluation design. Expected delivery scale. Estimated project budget. Final evaluation arrangements will be agreed collaboratively during the co-design phase with YEF and the appointed evaluator. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for funding, applicants must meet the following requirements: Location The intervention must be delivered in England. Eligible Organizations Applicants must be one of the following: Registered charity Registered company Statutory body Community Interest Company (CIC) Intervention Requirements Projects must: Focus on early targeted support. Address inappropriate and problematic attitudes and behaviours regarding gender norms and relationships. Operate primarily within educational settings. Support children and young people aged 10–18 years (or up to age 19 in SEND settings). Demonstrate a clear educational link. Improve knowledge, attitudes, or behaviours that contribute to preventing violence against women and girls. Be sufficiently developed for evaluation. Include an emerging Theory of Change. Organizational Capacity Applicants must demonstrate: Relevant experience. Strong partnerships where appropriate. Ability to deliver the proposed intervention successfully. Understanding of the target population and educational context. Evaluation Commitment Applicants must agree to: Participate in independent evaluation. Collaborate throughout co-design. Support delivery and evaluation activities. Click HERE to register. Application Process Interested organizations must submit: Completed application form. Completed budget template. All required supporting information. Applications should be emailed to: grants@youthendowmentfund.org.uk Subject line: VAWG Prevention Programme – Objective 2 Applicants should carefully review the official application guidance before submitting their proposals. Information Webinar Organizations interested in applying can attend an information webinar to better understand the funding opportunity and ask questions. Webinar Details Date: Thursday, 23 July 2026 Time: 1:30 PM – 3:00 PM A recording will be published on the Youth Endowment Fund website for those unable to attend. Funding Timeline Activity Date Funding call opens 6 July 2026 Application deadline 9:00 AM, Wednesday, 2 September 2026 Shortlisting notification 16 September 2026 Interviews 22 September – 9 October 2026 Committee review Early November 2026 Co-design phase begins From November 2026 Delivery and evaluation Following co-design Application Deadline 9:00 AM (BST), Wednesday, 2 September 2026 Late applications are unlikely to be considered. To learn more about the opportunity visit HERE and Apply. Why Organizations Should Apply Successful applicants will benefit from: Funding for intervention delivery. Independent evaluation support. Opportunity to strengthen evidence of programme effectiveness. Contribution to national violence prevention strategies. Collaboration with the Youth Endowment Fund and government partners. Increased credibility through rigorous evaluation. Potential to scale evidence-based interventions in the future. Conclusion The Youth Endowment Fund s Violence Against Women and Girls Prevention Programme – Objective 2 represents a significant opportunity for organizations committed to preventing harmful attitudes and behaviours among children and young people. By investing in evidence-informed interventions delivered within educational settings, the programme aims to build stronger prevention systems, improve safeguarding practices, and contribute to long-term reductions in violence against women and girls. Organizations with established interventions and the capacity to participate in independent evaluation are encouraged to prepare high-quality applications before the 9:00 AM, Wednesday, 2 September 2026 deadline. For more Grants opportunity from OFY CLICK HERE . title: Data Science Hackathon Programme (DSHP) Ghana Regional Hackathon 2026: Apply to Build AI and Machine Learning Skills Through a Real-World Challenge
summary: The Data Science Hackathon Programme (DSHP) Ghana Regional Hackathon 2026 is now accepting applications from university students and young professionals who are passionate about data science, artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, and technology-driven innovation. Organized under the Hack4dev Programme , this intensive four-day regional hackathon offers participants an opportunity to strengthen their technical expertise while working alongside like-minded innovators to solve real-world challenges using machine learning techniques and real datasets. If you are looking to gain practical experience beyond the classroom, expand your professional network, receive mentorship from experienced experts, and become part of an international data science community, this hackathon provides an excellent platform to accelerate your journey. About the Data Science Hackathon Programme (DSHP) The Data Science Hackathon Programme (DSHP) forms part of the broader Hack4dev Programme , an initiative dedicated to strengthening data science and artificial intelligence capacity through collaborative learning and practical problem-solving. Unlike traditional classroom learning, Hack4dev focuses on experiential education where participants work together to address real development challenges using modern data science tools and machine learning methodologies. During the Ghana Regional Hackathon, participants will spend four days immersed in an engaging learning environment where they will collaborate in multidisciplinary teams, analyze datasets, develop predictive models, evaluate their solutions, and present their findings before mentors and experts. The programme is designed not only to improve participants technical capabilities but also to enhance teamwork, communication, creativity, and analytical thinking—skills that are increasingly sought after across industries worldwide. Why Participate in the Ghana Regional Hackathon 2026? Data science and artificial intelligence continue to transform industries including healthcare, agriculture, finance, education, environmental management, logistics, and public policy. The Ghana Regional Hackathon offers participants the opportunity to gain practical exposure to these technologies while addressing meaningful challenges that have real societal impact. Rather than working on hypothetical assignments, participants will engage with authentic datasets and practical machine learning problems that mirror challenges encountered by professionals in industry and research. This practical experience helps bridge the gap between academic learning and workplace expectations. What Participants Will Do During the Hackathon Throughout the four-day programme, selected participants will work closely with mentors and fellow participants to complete various stages of a machine learning project. Activities include: Understanding the problem statement. Exploring real-world datasets. Cleaning and preparing data for analysis. Applying machine learning techniques. Developing predictive models. Evaluating model performance. Improving solutions through collaboration. Presenting technical findings and recommendations. Receiving feedback from experienced mentors. The programme encourages interdisciplinary collaboration, allowing participants from different academic and professional backgrounds to contribute diverse perspectives toward solving complex problems. Skills You Will Develop Participants will strengthen both technical and professional competencies throughout the hackathon. Technical Skills Participants will gain practical experience in: Data preparation and preprocessing. Python programming for data science. Machine learning model development. Data analysis. Model evaluation techniques. Working with real datasets. Artificial intelligence applications. Technical presentations. Professional Skills Beyond technical knowledge, participants will also improve their: Teamwork. Leadership. Communication. Problem-solving. Critical thinking. Project management. Collaboration across disciplines. Time management. Presentation skills. These transferable skills are highly valuable for future academic research, graduate studies, internships, and employment opportunities. Benefits of Participating Successful applicants will enjoy numerous benefits throughout the programme, including: Opportunity to solve a real-world machine learning challenge. Practical hands-on experience in data science and artificial intelligence. Guidance and mentorship from experienced trainers and professionals. Collaboration with multidisciplinary teams. Enhanced technical and analytical skills. Improved teamwork and communication abilities. Exposure to practical applications of machine learning. Networking with fellow students, professionals, and experts. Membership in the growing Hack4dev community. Experience that can strengthen future academic and professional applications. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to motivated students and young professionals who meet the following eligibility requirements. Applicants should: Be currently enrolled at a university as a final-year undergraduate student or postgraduate student. Or be a young professional interested in data science and AI. Demonstrate interest in data science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, or related disciplines. Have basic programming knowledge in Python. Be enthusiastic about learning. Enjoy working collaboratively within teams. Be available to attend the complete four-day hackathon. Be able to commute to and from the venue each day. Be willing to complete all pre-hackathon preparation activities. The organizers particularly encourage female applicants to submit applications in support of increased gender diversity within data science and artificial intelligence. APPLY HERE Important Participation Requirements Applicants should carefully note the following conditions before applying: Participants must provide their own laptop. Attendance throughout the entire hackathon is mandatory. Participants are expected to complete all assigned pre-hackathon learning materials. Participants require approval from their supervisors before attending. Travel expenses will not be covered. Accommodation expenses will not be covered. Participants must arrange their own daily transportation to and from the venue. What You Need Before the Event To participate successfully, applicants should have: A personal laptop suitable for programming. Basic Python programming knowledge. Interest in data science and artificial intelligence. Commitment to active participation. Availability for the entire event. Willingness to prepare before the hackathon begins. Why This Hackathon Matters As organizations increasingly rely on data-driven decision-making, demand for professionals with machine learning and artificial intelligence skills continues to rise. Participating in the DSHP Ghana Regional Hackathon enables students and young professionals to gain practical experience in solving authentic problems using modern computational techniques. The collaborative nature of the programme also allows participants to learn from peers with different backgrounds while receiving expert guidance throughout the process. Whether participants intend to pursue careers in technology, research, healthcare, finance, government, engineering, or entrepreneurship, the skills acquired during this hackathon can significantly strengthen their future opportunities. Female Applicants Are Strongly Encouraged The organizers are committed to promoting greater participation of women in science, technology, engineering, artificial intelligence, and data science. Female students and young professionals who meet the eligibility requirements are particularly encouraged to apply and contribute their perspectives to collaborative innovation. Application Deadline No application deadline has been stated in the announcement. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before registrations close. Conclusion The Data Science Hackathon Programme (DSHP) Ghana Regional Hackathon 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for aspiring data scientists, AI enthusiasts, university students, and young professionals to develop practical skills through immersive, hands-on learning. By working on real machine learning challenges, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, and receiving mentorship from experienced professionals, participants will gain valuable experience that extends far beyond traditional classroom education. If you are eager to expand your technical knowledge, strengthen your professional skills, and become part of the growing Hack4dev community, this hackathon offers an ideal environment to learn, innovate, and contribute to solving real-world challenges through data science. title: Paid Internship in Thailand 2026: Apply for the Just Transitions Incubator (JUTI) Project Team Internship for Bangkok Climate Action Week (BKKCAW 2026)
summary: Climate change continues to shape the future of communities, economies, and ecosystems across the globe. As countries accelerate efforts toward environmental sustainability and climate resilience, opportunities for young professionals to contribute to meaningful climate initiatives are becoming increasingly valuable. The Just Transitions Incubator (JUTI) is inviting applications for its Paid Project Team Internship supporting the organization of Bangkok Climate Action Week (BKKCAW) 2026 , one of Southeast Asia s leading climate action events. This internship provides recent graduates with an excellent opportunity to gain practical experience in project management, climate action programming, stakeholder coordination, venue research, and event planning while working alongside experienced professionals dedicated to building a more sustainable future. If you are passionate about environmental sustainability, climate advocacy, collaborative problem-solving, and gaining hands-on professional experience, this internship could be an excellent stepping stone for your career. Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 About Just Transitions Incubator (JUTI) Just Transitions Incubator (JUTI) is an organization committed to accelerating the transition toward a fair, inclusive, and climate-safe future. Through collaboration with communities, organizations, institutions, and innovators, JUTI develops initiatives that create lasting environmental and social impact. The organization operates with three core principles: Care Courage Collaboration These principles guide its mission of supporting breakthrough solutions that: Promote climate resilience. Strengthen local communities. Respect indigenous and local knowledge. Restore ecosystems. Build partnerships that create sustainable futures. Encourage innovative climate solutions across Southeast Asia and beyond. About Bangkok Climate Action Week (BKKCAW 2026) As part of this internship, selected candidates will contribute directly to the planning and implementation of Bangkok Climate Action Week 2026 (BKKCAW 2026) . BKKCAW has become one of Southeast Asia s premier climate action platforms, bringing together: Climate experts Policymakers Researchers Community leaders Youth organizations Civil society organizations Businesses Environmental advocates International partners The event focuses on encouraging collaborative climate solutions that support a greener, more inclusive, and climate-resilient future for the region. Interns will have the opportunity to contribute to real-world projects that support this major international climate initiative. Internship Overview Details Information Organization Just Transitions Incubator (JUTI) Position Paid Project Team Intern Number of Positions 1 Internship Type Paid Internship Work Schedule Monday to Friday (Hybrid) Reporting To Senior Project Coordinator Internship Duration July to October 2026 Start Date As soon as possible Monthly Allowance 15,000 THB Location Bangkok, Thailand Application Deadline 31 July 2026 What You Will Be Doing Interns will work closely with the Project Team while supporting the planning and delivery of Bangkok Climate Action Week 2026. The role combines research, coordination, project management, stakeholder engagement, and logistical planning. 1. Program Curation Support Interns will help shape the content and structure of BKKCAW 2026. Responsibilities include: Researching climate-related topics and emerging trends. Assisting with brainstorming sessions for conference themes. Supporting the development of engaging event programmes. Preparing session outlines. Assisting with speaker briefing documents. Helping create programme schedules. Maintaining organized databases of: Potential speakers Community organizations Government partners NGOs Environmental stakeholders Collaborating institutions 2. Venue Research and Coordination Another major responsibility involves identifying suitable venues throughout Bangkok for various BKKCAW activities. Interns will: Conduct venue research across Bangkok. Compare venue facilities and capacities. Gather quotations from venue providers. Evaluate venue suitability for different programme activities. Coordinate communication with venue representatives. Assist with logistics planning. Participate in venue inspections and site visits when necessary. 3. General Project Coordination Interns will also assist the Project Manager with day-to-day project operations. Responsibilities include: Supporting project administration. Coordinating meetings. Preparing presentations. Taking meeting notes. Organizing documentation. Updating project timelines. Tracking project progress. Managing spreadsheets using Microsoft Excel. Collaborating with multiple internal and external partners. Who Should Apply? JUTI is looking for motivated graduates who are eager to develop practical experience while contributing to meaningful environmental initiatives. Ideal candidates are individuals who: Enjoy collaborative work. Learn quickly. Take initiative. Have excellent attention to detail. Can manage multiple responsibilities. Are passionate about environmental sustainability. Want to contribute to climate solutions that create lasting social impact. Rather than focusing solely on academic background, JUTI values curiosity, dedication, and a genuine interest in climate action. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following criteria: Be a recent graduate from a Bachelor s degree programme. Graduates from any academic discipline are welcome. Hold Thai nationality. Be eligible to work in Thailand. Demonstrate a genuine interest in environmental sustainability and climate change. Possess good written and spoken Thai and English language skills. Be proactive and willing to learn. Be hardworking and adaptable. Work effectively in collaborative environments. Required Skills Successful applicants should possess many of the following skills: Technical Skills Microsoft Excel Microsoft Office Suite Google Workspace Canva Basic project management tools Professional Skills Research skills Organization Time management Communication Interpersonal skills Stakeholder coordination Attention to detail Documentation Administrative support Personal Qualities JUTI is seeking candidates who are: Curious Reliable Responsible Collaborative Flexible Passionate about sustainability Willing to work hard Motivated to develop new skills Internship Benefits Selected interns will receive numerous professional development opportunities, including: Monthly allowance of 15,000 THB . Hands-on experience working on one of Southeast Asia s major climate events. Exposure to project management and event coordination. Experience working with climate professionals and community partners. Practical knowledge of stakeholder engagement. Opportunity to strengthen communication and organizational skills. Experience in venue planning and logistics. Networking opportunities within the climate and sustainability sector. Professional mentoring while working alongside experienced project coordinators. Valuable experience that can strengthen future career opportunities in sustainability, NGOs, international development, and project management. Working Arrangement Successful applicants will work: Monday to Friday. Hybrid working arrangement. Based in Bangkok, Thailand. Reporting directly to the Senior Project Coordinator. Internship period running from July to October 2026. Candidates are encouraged to start as soon as possible. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare: An updated CV. A brief motivation letter explaining their interest in the internship and climate action. Submit your applications via email to: info@juti.earth with the subject line: Application for Paid Intern BKKCAW 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early submission is strongly encouraged. Important Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 31 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date, as applications will be reviewed as they are received. Learn more about the Just Transitions Incubator(JUTI) Team Internship for Bangkok Climate Action Week Conclusion The Just Transitions Incubator (JUTI) Paid Project Team Internship 2026 offers recent graduates an outstanding opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to one of Southeast Asia s most significant climate initiatives. Beyond receiving a monthly allowance, interns will build valuable skills in project management, stakeholder engagement, programme development, venue coordination, research, and environmental advocacy. Working on Bangkok Climate Action Week 2026 provides direct exposure to collaborative climate action and allows participants to contribute to meaningful initiatives that support sustainable development across the region. For recent graduates in Thailand who are passionate about climate change, environmental sustainability, and creating positive social impact, this internship represents an excellent opportunity to launch a career in the climate and development sector. Interested applicants should ensure they submit their CV and motivation letter before 31 July 2026 to be considered.
